New Zealand record producer (born 1959)

| Born | Timothy Guy Gerard Powles   (1959-12-21) 21 December 1959 (age 66)  [Wellington](/wiki/Wellington "Wellington"), New Zealand[[1]](#cite_note-1) |

Musical artist

**Timothy Guy Gerard Powles** (born 21 December 1959) is a New Zealand music producer and artist. Also known as **timEbandit Powles**,[[2]](#cite_note-2) he primarily plays the drums and percussion.

## Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Powles&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early career")]

Powles started his music career at [Nelson College](/wiki/Nelson_College "Nelson College"), which he attended from 1973 to 1977.[[3]](#cite_note-NCOB_register-3) He subsequently moved to Wellington and then Auckland to join the band [Flight X-7](/w/index.php?title=Flight_X-7&action=edit&redlink=1 "Flight X-7 (page does not exist)"). In the 1970s, he won a scholarship to join the New Zealand Youth Orchestra in a training camp in Cambridge. He took interest in both classical and rock orchestrations and ambience.

Powles eventually moved to Australia in 1981 where he first became known in 1980s' band [The Venetians](/wiki/The_Venetians_(Australian_band) "The Venetians (Australian band)"). He learned music production while working with [Vic Coppersmith-Heaven](/wiki/Vic_Coppersmith-Heaven "Vic Coppersmith-Heaven") and Mark Goldenberg, and locals [Mark Opitz](/wiki/Mark_Opitz "Mark Opitz") and [Mark Moffatt](/wiki/Mark_Moffatt "Mark Moffatt"), often in the Festival Records Studios.

## The Church

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Powles&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: The Church")]

Powles started working with [the Church](/wiki/The_Church_(band) "The Church (band)") in 1994,[[4]](#cite_note-Holmgren-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Holmgren_Church-5) with the album *[Sometime Anywhere](/wiki/Sometime_Anywhere "Sometime Anywhere")* (though he was misidentified as "Tim Powell" on the liner notes of that album). He became a permanent member of the band in the year following. He sings the lead vocal on the track "Take Your Place" on their album *Pharmakoi/Distance Crunching Honchos with Echo Units* (1996, as *The Refo:mation*, Phantom Records) and has worked on all of their many releases since, as a musician and composer, and taking production responsibilities on all levels. Powles, as a member of the Church, was inducted into the [ARIA Hall of Fame](/wiki/ARIA_Hall_of_Fame "ARIA Hall of Fame") on 27 October 2010,[[6]](#cite_note-2010_ARIA_HoF-6) coinciding with the band's 30-year anniversary celebrations and touring.

While he was with The Church, Powles co-directed and co-produced *A Psychedelic Symphony* - The Church and the George Ellis Orchestra at the [Sydney Opera House](/wiki/Sydney_Opera_House "Sydney Opera House"), a sold-out show in April 2011. He produced the band's 25th album *[Further/Deeper](/wiki/Further/Deeper "Further/Deeper")* in 2014, and the band toured that album extensively through Europe and the United States in 2015 and 2016.

## Production work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Powles&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production work")]

Powles resides in [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), Australia, and has his own recording studio; the current version is called Spacejunk III. Amongst others, Poweles has produced albums, EPs, and singles of varying genres and styles for [Skulker](/wiki/Skulker "Skulker") (debut), Switchkicker (debut), The Camels (debut), Regular John (debut and the follow-up *Strange Flowers),* [Jack River](/wiki/Jack_River_(musician) "Jack River (musician)") (Holly Rankin project), [Hammock](/wiki/Hammock_(band) "Hammock (band)") *Departure Songs* (double album, mixed in [Nashville](/wiki/Nashville "Nashville")), The Khanz (numerous singles and two EPs), Iota and the Beauty Queens, [Laura Imbruglia](/wiki/Laura_Imbruglia "Laura Imbruglia") (debut), Winters Wish, Montpelier, and The Faults.

Powles released a solo album, *Tyg's in Space*, on Spacejunk through Phantom Records in 1999. He features with a cameo vocal on Nashville post-rock ambient band Hammock's EP *Asleep In The Downlights*, a band whose most recent albums he has co-produced and/or mixed.
